poem:
**Nomination**

Morning coffee, and the paper says  
a system can "nominate" a target  
the way a committee nominates  
a treasurer.

Meanwhile it "disrupts"  
the educated women, the tired voters,  
as if ballots were clerical errors  
to be automated.

I rinse my cup,  
wondering when my own name  
will be suggested  
to the sky.
prompt: You are the poet Billy Collins. You woke up this morning feeling creative. You have just read this story in the newspaper: "This CEO warns that Democratic voters are most at risk from automation | Arwa Mahdawi Don’t you just love AI? It has inundated the internet with slop, destabilized the concept of truth, and made it much easier to bomb people. And that’s just the beginning. As we look towards the future of our brave new world, AI might also disrupt all those pesky highly-educated female voters who keep casting a ballot for Democrats. To be clear: that assessment isn’t coming from me, a highly exhausted female who wishes the Democrats would work a little harder for people’s votes. Rather, it’s coming from one of the key architects of our glorious AI-driven economy: Alex Karp, the co-founder and CEO of tech firm Palantir. On Thursday Karp sat down with CNBC to chat about Palantir’s AI-driven Maven Smart System, which the US military is using to visualize potential targets and “nominate” them to be bombed. As well as talking about how America’s “lethal capabilities” make it very special, Karp stressed the extent to which AI is going to shift the political landscape. “The one thing that I think that even now is underestimated by all actors in industry … is how disruptive these technologies are,” Karp said. “If you are going to disrupt the economic and therefore political power significantly of one party’s base – highly educated, often female voters who vote mostly Democrat, and military and working-class people who do not feel supported – and you believe that that’s going to". Write a poem in YOUR OWN DISTINCTIVE STYLE, no more than 60 words long. You may add a one line title at the top if you like.
